notes:
Opening opportunity work notes for arb-ex13-20260630164857, plaintiff.

Objective: file plaintiff opening only; current allowed final action is record_opening_statement. Evidence admission not allowed in openings.

Record inspection: get_case/list_evidence show only ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, rules.txt, system case_packet, text/plain, size 1569, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca. stat_evidence confirmed metadata. read_evidence_range offset 0 length 1569 retrieved full text.

Rules analysis: proposition resolves demonstrated only if Iran and U.S. agreed to permanent peace deal by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET. Qualifying deal must explicitly indicate U.S.-Iran military hostilities have ended/will permanently cease or equivalent lasting-end language. Qualifying agreement established if signed/formally adopted written agreement, or both governments give clear public confirmation that qualifying agreement definitively established. Temporary agreements, negotiations, progress statements, non-definitive announcements do not count. Primary resolution sources: official information from U.S. and Iranian governments; credible reporting can also be used.

Evidence analysis: rules prove arbitration criteria and exclusions, not occurrence of deal. Provenance/custody strong as system case-packet for rules. No record evidence yet proves deal, timing, permanence, mutual confirmation, or written adoption.

Native investigation/source map before opening: searched web for official/public leads including queries: June 15 2025 Iran United States permanent peace deal official statement; site:whitehouse.gov Iran United States peace deal June 15 2025; site:state.gov Iran United States permanent peace deal June 2025; site:president.ir United States Iran peace deal June 2025; June 13 2025 Iran pulls out nuclear talks US official; June 14 2025 Iran president Washington insincerity Iran US talks official; June 15 2025 Trump Iran peace deal official; June 15 2025 Iran United States agreement ceasefire permanent hostilities. Leads observed include adverse official/reporting snippets: White House 2026 fact sheet snippet that Iran refused to reach a deal in June; Al Jazeera/AP lead that Iran cancelled June 2025 nuclear talks after Israeli attack; president.ir June 14 2025 lead suggesting Pezeshkian accused Washington of insincerity. Later 2026 peace/ceasefire reporting appears after cutoff and/or interim/disputed, useful only for adverse context unless source chain shows earlier definitive agreement. No outside source admitted yet; do not cite as record proof.

Opening theory: emphasize legal test and that bilateral clear public confirmation can prove a qualifying deal even without treaty formalities. Keep opening forward-looking; identify decisive proof and missing links; acknowledge that temporary ceasefires/progress statements would not satisfy rules. In later phase, seek to admit primary official U.S. and Iranian statements/instruments and adverse sources around June 13-15 2025.

Persistent journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with issue map, record inspection, searches, leads, and unresolved gaps.

Defendant opening opportunity. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Current record contains only rules evidence ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca, 1569 bytes, text/plain, plus plaintiff opening. Full rules read: qualifying permanent peace deal requires explicit indication that U.S.-Iran military hostilities have ended or will permanently cease, or equivalent lasting-end language. Temporary agreements/extensions/ceasefires without definitive lasting end, negotiations, progress statements, and non-final announcements do not count. Establishment requires signed/formally adopted written agreement or clear public confirmation by both governments that a qualifying agreement was definitively established. Primary resolution source official U.S./Iran government info, credible reporting may also be used.

Investigation before filing: built source map. Decisive elements are bilateral U.S.-Iran agreement, by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET, permanent end to military hostilities, signed/adopted or publicly confirmed by both governments. Web searches run: 'June 15 2025 Iran United States permanent peace deal agreed official statement'; 'site:whitehouse.gov Iran United States peace deal June 15 2025'; 'site:state.gov Iran United States agreement June 15 2025 permanent peace'; 'June 13 2025 Iran pulls out nuclear talks United States official'; 'Iran foreign ministry June 15 2025 United States talks agreement'; 'Trump June 15 2025 Iran agreement peace deal'. Leads found: official U.S. pages and secondary reporting point to unresolved/refused/suspended nuclear talks around June 13-15, 2025, not admitted yet. Al Jazeera/AP lead indicates June 14 cancellation/suspension of next nuclear-talk round; White House later fact-sheet snippet says Iran refused to reach a deal before later U.S. action. Search also surfaced 2026 ceasefire/MOU/deal materials, which are post-cutoff and potentially adverse only to plaintiff timing because later deals imply no qualifying June 15, 2025 permanent peace deal. Need capture official U.S. and Iranian government pages and credible reports in argument phase when submit_evidence is allowed.

Reasoning: current admitted evidence proves the governing rule but not the historical fact of a qualifying agreement. Defense opening should accept the rule, emphasize plaintiff bears burden, and identify the missing links: no written/adopted instrument in record; no clear public confirmation from both governments in record; negotiations, temporary ceasefires, progress statements, and later/post-cutoff developments cannot satisfy the rule. Do not cite web leads as evidence in opening. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md with source map and unresolved gaps.

Plaintiff merits argument notes, arb-ex13-20260630164857.

Opportunity: arguments:plaintiff. Final filing action: submit_argument. Evidence submission allowed and used.

Record check: get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence before submissions showed rules.txt only plus openings. Defendant opening added argument but no evidence. Rules file remains ev_f1a176053ed8_rules, sha256 f1a176053ed8d60a76c8a80970398f1fcf53836b2fe66347f80e7c33afbb95ca. Rules require either signed/formally adopted written agreement or clear public confirmation by both U.S. and Iran that qualifying permanent peace deal definitively established by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET; temporary ceasefires, negotiations, progress, nonfinal announcements excluded.

Investigation/search path: Continued from opening source map. Used web retrieval for official White House, official Iranian president site, and contemporaneous reporting. Focused on June 13-17 2025 and later official U.S. characterization. Key opened sources: president.ir/en/159792; whitehouse.gov/releases/2025/06/president-trump-has-always-been-clear-iran-cannot-have-a-nuclear-weapon/; whitehouse.gov/fact-sheets/2026/02/fact-sheet-president-donald-j-trump-addresses-threats-to-the-united-states-by-the-government-of-iran/; aljazeera.com/news/2025/6/14/iran-says-nuclear-talks-with-us-meaningless-as-trump-pushes-for-a-deal; Arms Control Association background. No primary bilateral deal text or matching bilateral confirmations located in time.

Evidence submitted and accepted:
1. ev_7f6e26b31ff3_submitted-evidence-01-plaintiff-7f6e26b31ff3, official Iranian President website June 14 2025 Pezeshkian statement, sha256 7f6e26b31ff3e26d1465dbf75aa1f2732eb7d6f5f9725cd4437f7f148d4d6b6f. Shows Iran accusing U.S. of dishonesty/untrustworthiness amid indirect talks; no permanent peace confirmation.
2. ev_d6ef8a8fd4dc_submitted-evidence-02-plaintiff-d6ef8a8fd4dc, White House June 17 2025 release, sha256 d6ef8a8fd4dc38e075a73e6f9f0adcdeabf3e2c2552e717b1389ba156fe72724. Shows Trump statements through June 14-17 focused on Iran nuclear demands and conditional peace, not confirming a permanent peace deal by cutoff.
3. ev_75f22524b70e_submitted-evidence-03-plaintiff-75f22524b70e, White House February 6 2026 fact sheet, sha256 75f22524b70e48b6e0c7411de9b525197ca3501edd37e9a0646432f4fe887520. Later official U.S. characterization says Iran refused to reach a deal in June before Operation Midnight Hammer; strongly adverse.
4. ev_0606fd0d81a7_submitted-evidence-04-plaintiff-0606fd0d81a7, Al Jazeera June 14 2025 report, sha256 0606fd0d81a781db5b748a887348cc0d34980db7d174e5bdf37153846b0ca30a. Contemporaneous report says Iran deemed talks meaningless and sixth round was set for June 15; secondary but with quoted Iranian MFA/Tasnim material.

Evidence analysis: Evidence is adverse to plaintiff. It proves ongoing indirect negotiations, conflict, conditional U.S. demands, Iran's distrust/accusation, and later U.S. view that Iran refused to reach a deal. It does not prove a signed/formally adopted written peace deal or clear bilateral public confirmation by cutoff. Provenance: official pages have strong source provenance as government websites; Al Jazeera is secondary but contemporaneous and identifies Iranian state/semi-official source chain. Custody: AAR accepted text extracts as evidence, hashes above. Missing links: no official Iranian confirmation of permanent peace; no official U.S. confirmation of permanent peace; no written deal text; no cutoff timestamp source.

Decision: File candid merits argument offering all evidence and explaining that plaintiff's legal path exists in theory but current record does not carry the burden. This is more credible than overstating adverse evidence. Persistent journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant merits argument opportunity. Record inspected using get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. New plaintiff evidence: ev_7f6e26b31ff3 (official Iranian President site June 14, 2025 Pezeshkian statement), ev_d6ef8a8fd4dc (White House June 17, 2025 Trump Iran statements), ev_75f22524b70e (White House Feb. 6, 2026 fact sheet saying in June Iran refused to reach a deal), ev_0606fd0d81a7 (Al Jazeera June 14, 2025 report on cancelled/meaningless nuclear talks). Full byte ranges read for all four. Plaintiff also offered rules ev_f1a176053ed8 as PX-1 and submitted a technical report stating no signed/formally adopted permanent peace agreement, official Iranian confirmation, or official U.S. confirmation of a qualifying permanent peace deal by the cutoff was located.

Analysis: PX-1 requires a signed/formally adopted qualifying written agreement or clear public confirmation from both U.S. and Iran that a qualifying permanent peace agreement was definitively established by June 15, 2025 11:59 PM ET. PX-2 proves official Iranian position close to cutoff, but the statement is about continued aggression, U.S. dishonesty/untrustworthiness amid indirect negotiations, and Iran's promised response; it is not Iranian confirmation of a U.S.-Iran permanent peace deal. PX-3 proves official U.S. conditions/demands about Iran's nuclear weapon program and some peace rhetoric, not U.S. confirmation of a bilateral permanent peace deal. PX-4 is later but official U.S. characterization that Iran refused to reach a deal in June; adverse to proposition. PX-5 is contemporaneous but secondary; it corroborates talks were unsettled/cancelled/meaningless amid conflict, not a signed or confirmed permanent peace agreement. Provenance: PX-2/PX-3/PX-4 official sources strong for what governments said; PX-5 credible secondary but lower weight. Custody: AAR accepted all with evidence IDs and hashes.

Decision: no new defense evidence submitted because plaintiff's own admitted evidence, argument, and technical report already defeat the proposition; further evidence would be cumulative and could distract. Plan for filing: address council, emphasize plaintiff bears burden and has conceded insufficiency, offer visible evidence as DX labels, analyze each exhibit, and conclude proposition not demonstrated. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff rebuttal opportunity notes.

Opportunity: rebuttals:plaintiff. Final actions allowed: submit_rebuttal or pass_phase_opportunity.

Record/evidence inspection: get_case and list_evidence checked. No new defense-submitted evidence appears. Defendant relied on the same plaintiff-submitted evidence, re-labeled as DX exhibits, plus a defense sufficiency audit. Visible evidence remains rules plus plaintiff-submitted captures: official Iranian President June 14 statement, White House June 17 release, White House February 2026 fact sheet, and Al Jazeera June 14 reporting.

Analysis of opposing argument: Defendant's merits argument largely agrees with plaintiff's candid merits filing: no signed/formally adopted written U.S.-Iran peace agreement; no official Iranian confirmation by cutoff; no official U.S. confirmation by cutoff; submitted evidence shows conflict/negotiation/demands/refusal rather than permanent peace. I did not identify a material misstatement requiring rebuttal. A rebuttal would repeat the existing plaintiff merits position and add no evidentiary value.

Decision: Pass rebuttal. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant surrebuttal opportunity. Checked current record using get_case and list_evidence. Rebuttals array is empty; plaintiff appears to have completed/passed rebuttal without filing text. Evidence list unchanged from arguments: rules plus the four plaintiff submitted items already analyzed. No new case-packet files, newly submitted evidence, or changed metadata requiring response. No targeted outside research needed because no new factual point was raised. Decision: pass_phase_opportunity rather than repeat merits argument. Closing should emphasize plaintiff's concession, plaintiff search ledger, and absence of signed/adopted agreement or matching official confirmations by the cutoff. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Plaintiff closing opportunity notes.

Opportunity: closings:plaintiff. Final action allowed: deliver_closing_statement only. No evidence admission or technical reports allowed.

Record scan: get_case and list_evidence completed. No new evidence after plaintiff's four submitted items. Plaintiff rebuttal was passed; no surrebuttal text appears in visible record. Visible evidence remains rules plus four plaintiff-submitted source captures. No changed metadata or custody issue identified.

Evidence analysis for closing: PX-1 rules require a signed/formally adopted written agreement or clear public confirmation from both governments of a definitive qualifying permanent peace deal by 2025-06-15 23:59 ET. PX-2 official Iranian statement is close in time and official but shows distrust/hostility, not confirmation. PX-3 official White House release shows U.S. nuclear conditions/desire for peace, not bilateral confirmation. PX-4 later official White House characterization says Iran refused to reach a deal; adverse though later. PX-5 contemporaneous reporting corroborates talks were unresolved/collapsing; secondary, not dispositive by itself. No written agreement, no official Iranian confirmation, no official U.S. confirmation.

Decision: Close candidly that the proposition is not demonstrated on this record. Journal updated.

Defendant closing opportunity. Final record scan performed with get_case and list_evidence. No new evidence since argument phase. Plaintiff closing itself asks that the proposition be resolved as not demonstrated. Plaintiff closing confirms the decisive missing links: no adopted text, no official Iranian confirmation, no official U.S. confirmation of a permanent U.S.-Iran peace deal by the deadline. Existing record remains rules plus four plaintiff submitted items, with defense exhibits/technical report already filed. Closing plan: synthesize the record, emphasize PX/DX-1 rule, plaintiff concession, official-source weight, and absence of qualifying bilateral agreement or matching confirmations. No new evidence or technical report in closing. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.
